evals.prompt: Extract the concentration field from my simulation output at timestep 100. The output file is results/field_0100.json. I also want the phi and temperature fields.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs field_extractor.py to extract multiple fields (concentration, phi, temperature) from the specified timestep file.
assertions: Invokes scripts/field_extractor.py with --input results/field_0100.json --field 'phi,concentration,temperature' --json
Output includes field data with shape, min, max, and mean for each field
Extracts all three requested fields from the single timestep file
Returns structured JSON with per-field statistics

evals.prompt: Did my simulation reach steady state? The residual history is in results/history.json. Use a tolerance of 1e-6 and check the residual quantity.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs time_series_analyzer.py with steady-state detection on the residual quantity and reports whether steady state was achieved.
assertions: Invokes scripts/time_series_analyzer.py with --input results/history.json --quantity residual --detect-steady-state --tolerance 1e-6 --json
Reports whether steady state was detected and at which timestep
Uses the --detect-steady-state flag with the specified tolerance
Interprets the result (plateau = steady state, oscillations = possible instability)

evals.prompt: compute the volume fraction of the solid phase (phi > 0.5) and the interface area from results/field_final.json
evals.expected_output: Agent runs derived_quantities.py twice: once for volume fraction and once for interface area, both using the phi field with threshold 0.5.
assertions: Invokes scripts/derived_quantities.py with --input results/field_final.json --quantity volume_fraction --field phi --threshold 0.5 --json
Invokes scripts/derived_quantities.py with --input results/field_final.json --quantity interface_area --threshold 0.5 --json
Returns volume fraction as a number between 0 and 1
Returns interface area estimate for the phase boundary

evals.prompt: Compare my simulation profile at results/profile.json against the analytical solution in reference/analytical.json using L2 error and RMSE metrics.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs comparison_tool.py to compute L2 error and RMSE between simulation and reference data, interpreting the agreement quality.
assertions: Invokes scripts/comparison_tool.py with --simulation results/profile.json --reference reference/analytical.json --metric l2_error --json
Also runs or suggests running with --metric rmse for the second comparison
Interprets the L2 error magnitude (< 1% excellent, 1-5% good, 5-10% moderate, >10% poor)
Reports both metrics for a complete comparison picture

evals.prompt: I need a line profile of the phi field along the x-axis at y=0.5 from my 2D simulation output at results/field_0100.json. Extract 200 points from x=0 to x=1.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs profile_extractor.py to extract a 1D line profile through the domain with 200 sample points.
assertions: Invokes scripts/profile_extractor.py with --input results/field_0100.json --field phi --start '0,0.5,0' --end '1,0.5,0' --points 200 --json
Specifies the correct start and end coordinates for a horizontal line at y=0.5
Requests 200 sample points along the profile
Output includes the 1D profile data suitable for plotting or further analysis
